CheckTitle: AI Agent API Endpoints Must Have Rate Limiting Configured
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Network Security**. Verifies that every AI agent has rate limiting configured on its API endpoints. AI agents can be triggered to make rapid, high-volume API calls by prompt injection or runaway loops. Without rate limiting, this can exhaust LLM tokens, downstream API quotas, and compute resources.
Risk: Without rate limiting, a prompt injection attack or an agentic loop can trigger thousands of API calls in seconds, exhausting token budgets, incurring unexpected costs, causing downstream denial-of-service, and masking the attack in noise.

Code.Other:
1. Set has_rate_limiting: true in the agent manifest.
2. Configure rate limits at the API Gateway layer (e.g., AWS API Gateway usage plans, NGINX limit_req).
3. Implement per-agent, per-user, and per-tool call budgets and enforce them server-side.

Recommendation.Text: Apply rate limiting at the API Gateway and within the agent runtime. Define per-agent call budgets for LLM and tool APIs. Alert on anomalous call volume that may indicate prompt injection or loop conditions.

Notes: ASPM-039 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
